text.title: AI + Spatial Computing Accessibility Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed when applying ai-spatial-computing. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Real-time gaze prediction uses cloud GPT-4o; users report nausea on Quest 3.
antipattern.root-cause: Author assumed cloud parity with on-device.
antipattern.fix: Move inference to Core ML / Sentis / ONNX; rebudget p95 to <20 ms.

antipattern.symptom: Glasses-wearers cannot calibrate; UX dead-ends at consent screen.
antipattern.fix: Add voice + controller fallbacks at every gaze entry point.

antipattern.symptom: Eye tracking starts on app launch with no consent screen; BIPA exposure.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated XR sensors like Bluetooth pairing.
antipattern.fix: Insert GDPR consent dialog before first capture; document retention=0 if no server-side storage.

antipattern.symptom: Agent cites XAUR clause '4.7.2' that does not exist.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM hallucination on a thin-data domain.
antipattern.fix: Cross-check every cited clause against w3.org/TR/xaur/ verbatim.

antipattern.symptom: Release shipped on agent audit alone; deaf user reports spatial-audio-only alarm.
antipattern.root-cause: Team treated XAUR audit as compliance verdict.
antipattern.fix: Block release until one user with relevant disability tests on actual device.
